what_it_does: An integration test that exercises the shared Suite and the MCP agent face: it builds a graph via UI-side generic verbs, verifies the MCP face sees and runs the same persisted graph from the shared store, and checks that consolidated MCP tools/capabilities expose node creation, graph execution, and node-type discovery.
observations: Uses a persistent FsStore and a shared graph ID 'e5test'.
Defines a global ok flag and a check helper that prints PASS/FAIL.
Discovers node types from a local nodes directory.
Creates a two-node graph (constant -> uppercase) and asserts the graph exists.
Verifies unknown node types raise KeyError.
Imports mcp_face.server as a separate Suite instance sharing the same store.
Asserts MCP can list the graph, run it, and read the uppercase result 'HI'.
Checks capabilities()['node_types'] includes constant, uppercase, llm.
Verifies consolidated MCP tools include node, run_graph, capabilities (tolerates SDK variance).
Cleans up a prior graph file at start.
standouts: Demonstrates one substrate with two faces (UI Suite + MCP agent).
Asserts shared-store semantics by using two Suite instances.
Validates consolidated MCP tool contract (node/run_graph/capabilities) rather than flat list_types/create_node.
conventions: Test script runnable as __main__ with sys.exit(main()).
Uses sys.path insertion relative to file location.
Direct file-system cleanup of persisted graph before test.
Imports project modules via relative path manipulation.
concerns: Direct os.remove on a store-internal graph path couples test to FsStore layout.
Global mutable ok and check side-effect printing.
Tolerates len(names)==0 in tool check, which weakens the assertion.
Hardcoded node type set and result value 'HI' assume specific node behavior not defined in this file.
No teardown after test leaves graph in store.
notes: End-to-end test proving the Suite and MCP agent share one persisted graph substrate through generic create/run/capability verbs.
questions: What exact behavior do constant and uppercase nodes implement?
How is mcp_face.server initialized and does it reuse the same Suite instance?
What is the C7/N7 naming convention and where is it documented?
What does capabilities() return beyond node_types?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose ('E5 — shared Suite + MCP agent face, one brain two faces') matches the actual test flow: it builds a graph via Suite, then verifies the MCP face can see and run it.
apparent_themes: Shared substrate / single source of truth.
Generic verbs over flat specialized tools.
UI and agent as one system with two faces.
intention_signals: It is trying to validate architectural unity: that the same graph store and node registry power both the UI-facing Suite and the agent-facing MCP server, with node operations exposed generically through capabilities rather than one-off tools.
novelty: Distinct in explicitly testing dual-face convergence by instantiating the UI Suite and the MCP server as separate consumers of the same persisted graph, and in asserting a consolidated MCP tool contract.
contribution: Provides an integration-level assertion that the Suite graph builder, shared persistent store, and MCP agent face operate on the same graph substrate with generic verbs, which a fused system would keep as a living contract test.
summary_for_embedding: Integration test for the shared Suite and MCP agent face that builds a graph via generic verbs, then verifies the MCP server sees, runs, and reads results from the same persisted graph store.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that the UI Suite and MCP agent are one system sharing a single graph substrate and a consolidated generic-verb tool contract.

symbols.does: Prints a PASS/FAIL line and accumulates the result into the global ok flag.
symbols.description: A small test helper that takes a label and condition, updates the module-level ok boolean with the condition, and prints a formatted pass/fail line for the current assertion.

symbols.does: Creates a new Suite instance with a node registry discovered from the local nodes directory and the configured FsStore.
symbols.description: Factory function used to instantiate independent Suite objects (UI and potentially MCP) that share the same persistent file-system store and node type registry.

symbols.does: Runs the E5 integration test: builds a graph via Suite, verifies the MCP face shares the store, and checks consolidated tools/capabilities.
symbols.description: Cleans any prior e5test graph, constructs a two-node graph through generic Suite verbs, asserts error handling for unknown node types, then imports the MCP server face and validates it can list, run, and read results from the same graph while exposing the consolidated node/run_graph/capabilities tool contract.
